question_text: Which statement best explains why a knife with a sharp blade cuts easier than a blunt knife?

options.label: A
options.text: A sharp blade has a smaller area of contact, so the same force produces higher pressure

options.label: B
options.text: A sharp blade applies less force to the object being cut

options.label: C
options.text: A blunt knife has higher pressure because it covers a larger area

options.label: D
options.text: Sharp blades and blunt knives produce the same pressure

explanation: A sharp blade concentrates the force over a much smaller area. Since pressure = force ÷ area, a smaller area means higher pressure for the same force, making it easier to cut through materials.
